page.content: In the Clinical Trial Enrollment Rates and R&D Productivity Metrics for AbbVie as of January 2024, the overall enrollment comprises 50 total trials with 12,000 enrolled patients out of a target enrollment of 15,000, resulting in an enrollment rate of 80% and a dropout rate of 10%, while the completion ratio stands at 75%.

lines.text: In the Clinical Trial Enrollment Rates and R&D Productivity Metrics for AbbVie as of January 2024, the overall enrollment
comprises 50 total trials with 12,000 enrolled patients out of a target enrollment of 15,000, resulting in an enrollment rate of
80% and a dropout rate of 10%, while the completion ratio stands at 75%.

page.content: Of these trials, there are 20 Phase I trials and 30 Phase II trials, with 25 Phase III trials. Additionally, the total number of sites involved is 100, with patient diversity represented at 60% in the Asia-Pacific region. The average age of participants is 50 years, and the average completion time is 100 days for Phase I, 200 days for Phase II, and 200 days for Phase III. Cost metrics include total costs of $3,456,000, cost per patient at $1.2M, and an average budget of $1.2M. The funding breakdown shows public funding at 20%, private funding at 15%, and partnership funding at 5%. Regulatory approvals include 20 Phase I approvals, 15 Phase II approvals, and 2 Phase III approvals, leading to a total of 37 approvals and an overall approval rate of 70% with an average success rate of 85%. In R&D metrics, clinical development costs total CNY 3000 million, with a YoY change of 5% and a market share of 12%.

page.content: Preclinical costs are CNY 1500 million with a YoY change of 3% and a market share of 6%, while Phase I costs are CNY 800 million, Phase II at CNY 600 million, and Phase III at CNY 1000 million, leading to a total R&D spend of CNY 4900 million with a 6% YoY change and 20 new drug approvals.
